description: Presenting a brand or product as the direct solution to a specific customer problem or pain point, emphasizing immediate value and resolution.
structural_recipe: Lead with the customer's problem or frustration in visual or caption form. Show the product/service as the clear answer. Use problem-solution structure: 'When you [problem], [brand] [solution].' Include before/after imagery or contrast. Call to action emphasizing ease or relief. Keep language direct and benefit-focused rather than feature-focused.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers respond strongly to pragmatic, value-driven messaging that respects their time and intelligence. Solution-framing positions the brand as helpful and efficient rather than self-promotional. It aligns with preference for straightforward communication and demonstrates understanding of customer needs, building trust and relevance.
transplantation_caveats: Avoid overstating problems or creating unnecessary alarm—Saudi audiences value optimism and positive framing
Ensure solutions are authentically aligned with Islamic values and local market expectations
Problem framing should feel relatable rather than condescending or pitying
